title: Add token counting to Evolution Service
description: Add token counting for each LLM call in EvolutionService using tiktoken. Return token usage alongside evolved playbook.

title: Add anomaly detection for suspicious access patterns
description:
## Problem
No behavioral analysis to detect unusual access patterns that might indicate compromise or abuse.

## Solution
Add basic anomaly detection for suspicious activity.

## Patterns to detect
- Login from new geographic location
- Unusual time of access
- Rapid API key creation
- High volume of failed operations
- Accessing many different playbooks rapidly
- Sudden spike in usage

## Implementation
1. Track user behavior baselines (location, time, usage patterns)
2. Flag anomalies for review or automatic action
3. Send notification emails for suspicious activity
4. Consider temporary account lock pending verification

## Simpler alternative for MVP
- Just send email on login from new IP/location
- Alert yourself when any user's spend exceeds threshold

## Notes
This is nice-to-have. Focus on preventive measures first (email verification, spending caps).

title: Add CSRF protection for state-changing operations
description:
## Problem
No CSRF tokens for state-changing operations. Session-based endpoints (especially OAuth) may be vulnerable.

## Current mitigating factors
- API uses token-based auth primarily (not cookies)
- CORS configured to limit origins

## Solution
Add CSRF protection for any cookie/session-based flows.

## Implementation
1. Generate CSRF token on session creation
2. Require token in header or body for state-changing requests
3. Validate token matches session
4. Focus on OAuth flow which uses sessions

## Files to modify
- `ace_platform/api/routes/oauth.py`
- `ace_platform/api/middleware.py`

## Notes
Lower priority since most auth is token-based, but OAuth flow should be protected.

title: Add IP allowlist support for API keys
description:
## Problem
API keys can be used from any IP address. If a key is compromised, attacker has full access.

## Solution
Allow users to restrict API keys to specific IP addresses or ranges.

## Implementation
1. Add `allowed_ips` field to APIKey model (JSON array)
2. On API key creation, optionally specify allowed IPs/CIDRs
3. On each request, validate source IP against allowlist
4. If allowlist is empty, allow any IP (current behavior)

## UI/UX
- Show current IP to user when creating key
- Allow CIDR notation (e.g., 10.0.0.0/8)
- Warn if adding very broad ranges

## Files to modify
- `ace_platform/db/models.py` - add field to APIKey
- `ace_platform/api/auth.py` - validate IP on key auth
- `ace_platform/api/routes/auth.py` - accept IPs on creation

title: Add admin alerting for high-spend users
description:
## Problem
As an indie developer, you need to know immediately when something looks wrong - before costs spiral.

## Solution
Add simple alerting when users hit spend thresholds.

## Implementation
1. Add threshold checks in metering code
2. Alert when any user:
   - Hits 50% of their tier's cost limit
   - Hits 80% of their tier's cost limit
   - Has unusual spike vs their baseline
3. Send alert via:
   - Email to admin
   - Slack webhook (optional)
   - Log with high severity for monitoring

## Simple MVP
Just add a daily summary email:
- Total platform spend today
- Top 5 users by spend
- Any users who exceeded 50% of limit

## Files to modify
- `ace_platform/core/metering.py` - add threshold checks
- `ace_platform/workers/tasks.py` - add daily summary task
- Add email/notification utility

title: Write unit tests for Evolution Service

title: Set up MCP server core

title: Implement MCP authentication middleware
description:
Implement MCP authentication middleware for API keys:

1. Look up hashed key in database (bcrypt comparison)
2. Validate key is not revoked (revoked_at is null)
3. Check scopes for requested operation (read, write, evolve)
4. Update last_used_at timestamp on successful auth
5. Return 401/403 with clear error messages

API keys are generated via /auth/api-keys endpoints.

title: Implement get_playbook MCP tool
description:
Implement get_playbook MCP tool:

Parameters:
- playbook_id: str (required)
- section: str (optional) - filter to specific section
- version: int (optional) - get specific version (default: current)

Returns: Playbook content as structured text

Must enforce playbook ownership - only return playbooks owned by authenticated user or public starters.

title: Implement report_outcome MCP tool
description:
Implement report_outcome MCP tool:

Parameters:
- playbook_id: str (required)
- task_description: str (required)
- outcome: success|failure|partial (required)
- reasoning_trace: str (optional, max 10KB)
- notes: str (optional, max 2KB)

Returns: { outcome_id, status: recorded, pending_outcomes }

Enforce input size limits and playbook ownership.

title: Implement list_playbooks MCP tool
description:
Implement list_playbooks MCP tool:

Parameters:
- include_starters: bool (optional, default: true)

Returns: Array of { id, name, description, last_updated, bullet_count, outcome_count }

Filter to playbooks owned by authenticated user plus optional starter playbooks.

title: Implement trigger_evolution MCP tool
description:
Implement trigger_evolution MCP tool:

Parameters: playbook_id (required)
Returns: job_id, status, is_new

IMPORTANT: Must be idempotent - if a queued/running job exists, return that job instead of creating a new one. This prevents race conditions and duplicate evolution jobs.

title: Add MCP tools input validation and error handling
description: Add comprehensive input validation and meaningful error messages for all MCP tools

title: Create SQLAlchemy database models
description:
Create SQLAlchemy models for all entities:

Core entities:
- User (with is_active, email_verified, updated_at)
- Playbook (with current_version_id, status enum, source enum)
- PlaybookVersion (NEW - for version history)
- Outcome (with processed_at, evolution_job_id, reflection_data)
- EvolutionJob (with from/to version_id, token_totals, ace_core_version)
- UsageRecord (with model, split tokens, Decimal cost_usd, request_id, metadata)
- ApiKey (NEW - for MCP auth)

See docs/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md for full schema details.

Create database connection utilities in ace_platform/db/session.py:

1. Async engine + AsyncSession factory for API/MCP (using asyncpg)
2. Sync engine + Session factory for workers (using psycopg2-binary)
3. Shared model metadata that works with both session types
4. Connection pool configuration for each use case
5. Context managers for session lifecycle

title: Set up Celery with Redis backend

title: Create evolution worker task
description:
Create Celery evolution worker task with:

1. Sync database session (not async)
2. Row locking via SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on playbook
3. Atomic transaction for:
   - Create new PlaybookVersion
   - Update Playbook.current_version_id
   - Mark outcomes as processed with evolution_job_id
   - Update EvolutionJob status
4. Token usage logging to UsageRecord
5. Retry with exponential backoff
6. Idempotent behavior

title: Implement automatic evolution triggering
description:
Implement automatic evolution triggering based on thresholds:

Rules:
- Trigger after N unprocessed outcomes (default: 5)
- OR after T hours since last evolution with at least 1 outcome (default: 24h)
- Whichever condition is met first
- Outcomes arriving during a running job wait for next run

Implement as periodic Celery beat task.

Test full flow end-to-end:

1. Register new user
2. Create playbook
3. Generate API key
4. MCP: get_playbook
5. MCP: report_outcome (multiple)

description: Fix any issues discovered during E2E and load testing
notes:
PRIORITY NOTE: This is a review/catch-all task that should be completed as the VERY LAST task before launch. It must be done AFTER:
1. ALL other bd issues are resolved (including P2 and P3 issues)
2. Developer (Danny) has completed User Acceptance Testing (UAT)

This issue involves reviewing the entire system for any remaining issues discovered during E2E testing, load testing, and UAT.

title: Add PlaybookVersion model for evolution history
description:
Add PlaybookVersion table to track version history:
- id: UUID (primary key)
- playbook_id: UUID (foreign key, indexed)
- version_number: int
- content: text
- bullet_count: int
- created_at: datetime
- created_by_job_id: UUID (foreign key, nullable)
- diff_summary: str (nullable)

Update Playbook model to add current_version_id foreign key.

title: Add ApiKey model for MCP authentication
description:
Add ApiKey table for per-user MCP API keys:
- id: UUID (primary key)
- user_id: UUID (foreign key, indexed)
- name: str
- key_prefix: str (first 8 chars for identification)
- hashed_key: str (bcrypt hashed, never store plaintext)
- scopes: JSONB
- created_at: datetime
- last_used_at: datetime (nullable)
- revoked_at: datetime (nullable)

Keys must be hashed like passwords. Only show full key once at creation time.

title: Add partial unique index for evolution concurrency
description:
Add partial unique index on EvolutionJob table to prevent concurrent evolutions:

CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_evolution_job_active_per_playbook 
ON evolution_jobs (playbook_id) 
WHERE status IN ('queued', 'running');

This ensures only one active evolution job per playbook at a time.

title: Create .env.example with all required variables
description: Create .env.example file documenting all required environment variables for the platform

title: Implement get_evolution_status MCP tool
description:
Add new MCP tool to check evolution job status:

Parameters:
  - job_id: str (required)

Returns:
  - job_id: str
  - status: str (queued, running, completed, failed)
  - outcomes_processed: int
  - started_at: datetime
  - completed_at: datetime (nullable)
  - error_message: str (nullable)

Needed for client UX - allows polling for job completion.

title: Implement API key management endpoints
description:
Add CRUD endpoints for API key management:

POST   /auth/api-keys          - Create new API key (returns full key once)
GET    /auth/api-keys          - List user's API keys (prefix only)
DELETE /auth/api-keys/{id}     - Revoke API key

Keys are hashed with bcrypt. Only the key_prefix is shown after creation.
Support scopes: read, write, evolve.

title: Implement auth refresh endpoint
description:
Add JWT token refresh endpoint:

POST /auth/refresh
  - Accepts refresh token
  - Returns new access token
  - Access tokens expire in 30 minutes (configurable)
  - Refresh tokens expire in 7 days (configurable)

This enables long-lived sessions without storing long-lived access tokens.

title: Implement playbook versions endpoints
description:
Add endpoints for playbook version history:

GET /playbooks/{id}/versions      - List all versions (paginated)
GET /playbooks/{id}/versions/{v}  - Get specific version content

Enables viewing evolution history and comparing versions.
Each evolution creates a new version with diff_summary.

title: Implement outcome creation REST endpoint
description:
Add REST fallback for outcome reporting:

POST /playbooks/{id}/outcomes

  - outcome: success|failure|partial (required)
  - reasoning_trace: str (optional, max 10KB)
  - notes: str (optional, max 2KB)

Returns: { outcome_id, status: recorded, pending_outcomes }

Provides REST alternative to MCP report_outcome tool.

title: Set up structured JSON logging
description:
Implement structured logging infrastructure:

- JSON log format for production
- Request correlation IDs via middleware
- Log levels configurable per environment
- Ensure playbook content NOT logged by default
- Optional content truncation in logs

Create ace_platform/core/logging.py

title: Implement rate limiting
description:
Add rate limiting to protect abuse vectors:

- Login attempts: 5/minute per IP
- Outcome reporting: 100/hour per user
- Evolution triggering: 10/hour per playbook

Implement in ace_platform/core/security.py
Add middleware to ace_platform/api/middleware.py

title: Add input size validation
description:
Implement input size limits across API and MCP:

- Playbook content: 100KB max
- Reasoning trace: 10KB max

Add validation to both API endpoints and MCP tools.
Return clear error messages when limits exceeded.

Also add pre-commit hooks configuration.

Optional for MVP but recommended for production.

Uses Jinja2 templates consistent with other dashboard pages.

Add version history view to playbook detail page:

Uses Jinja2 templates.

title: Add request correlation ID middleware
description:
Add middleware to generate and propagate correlation IDs:

1. Generate UUID for each request if not present in headers
2. Add to response headers
3. Include in all log messages
4. Pass through to background tasks

Essential for debugging and tracing requests across services.

title: Add login attempt lockout
description:
Implement exponential backoff for failed login attempts:

- Track failed attempts per IP/email
- After N failures, increase wait time exponentially
- Consider using Redis for distributed rate limiting
- Reset counter on successful login

Part of security hardening.

title: Document environment variables
description:
Create comprehensive documentation of all environment variables:

- Required vs optional

Add to docs/ directory.

Create self-hosted deployment guide:

- Docker Compose setup

Target audience: developers wanting to run their own instance.

title: Test evolution idempotency
description:
Add E2E test for evolution idempotency:

1. Trigger evolution for a playbook
2. While job is queued/running, trigger again
3. Verify same job_id returned (is_new=false)
4. Verify no duplicate jobs created

Critical for preventing race conditions.

title: Test API key authentication
description:
Add tests for API key authentication flow:

1. Create API key, verify full key returned once
2. List keys, verify only prefix shown
3. Use key for MCP auth, verify access granted
4. Revoke key, verify access denied
5. Test scope enforcement

Critical for MCP security.

title: MCP server enters restart loop in Docker
description: During UAT testing, the MCP server container enters a restart loop with RuntimeWarning about module import order. The health endpoint at localhost:8001/health returns empty responses. Container logs show repeated RuntimeWarning messages. This needs investigation to identify the root cause and fix the import order issue.

title: Create Evolution Service wrapper
description: Create EvolutionService class in platform/core/evolution.py that wraps ace_core. Implement evolve_playbook(playbook_content, outcomes) -> new_playbook_content

title: initial_content causes MissingGreenlet error when creating playbook
description: When creating a playbook with initial_content parameter, the API returns a MissingGreenlet error. Root cause: sync/async mismatch when creating PlaybookVersion. Workaround: create playbook without initial_content.

title: Enforce email verification for protected routes
description:
## Implementation Complete

### Phase 1: Enforcement (Done)
- Updated `create_api_key` endpoint to require `VerifiedUser` instead of `RequiredUser`
- Added email verification check to MCP `trigger_evolution` tool
- Added email verification check to MCP `record_outcome` tool  
- Added email verification check to REST API `create_outcome` endpoint

### Phase 2: Email Flow (Done)
- Added Resend email service integration
- Created `ace_platform/core/email.py` with:
  - JWT-based verification tokens
  - `send_verification_email()` function
  - `send_welcome_email()` function
- Added new endpoints:
  - `POST /auth/send-verification-email` - resend verification email
  - `POST /auth/verify-email` - verify email with token
- Updated registration to send verification email on signup
- Added config settings for Resend API key and email settings
- Updated `.env.example` with email configuration
- Added `resend` to project dependencies

### OAuth Users
OAuth users (Google/GitHub) are automatically verified since their emails are verified by the provider.

### Files Modified
- `ace_platform/api/auth.py` - Added VerifiedUser import
- `ace_platform/api/routes/auth.py` - Email verification endpoints
- `ace_platform/api/routes/playbooks.py` - Outcome verification check
- `ace_platform/mcp/server.py` - MCP tool verification checks
- `ace_platform/core/email.py` - New email service module
- `ace_platform/config.py` - Email settings
- `.env.example` - Email configuration docs
- `pyproject.toml` - Added resend dependency

title: Add spending cap per subscription tier
description:
## Problem
Tier limits cap evolution runs but not actual LLM spend. A malicious user could craft expensive prompts and blow through the LLM budget.

## Solution
Add a hard spending limit per tier that blocks further LLM calls when exceeded.

## Implementation
1. Add `monthly_cost_limit_usd` to `TierLimits` dataclass in `ace_platform/core/limits.py`
2. Define limits per tier:
   - FREE: $1.00/month
   - STARTER: $20.00/month (2x their payment)
   - PRO: $60.00/month
   - ULTRA: $160.00/month
   - ENTERPRISE: configurable
3. Add `check_spending_limit()` function similar to `check_can_evolve()`
4. Call this check before any LLM operation

## Files to modify
- `ace_platform/core/limits.py`
- `ace_platform/core/llm_proxy.py` (or wherever LLM calls are made)

## Impact
Protects against runaway LLM costs from malicious or buggy usage.

title: Require card on file for free trial (Stripe setup intent)
description:
## Problem
Free tier has no friction - anyone can create unlimited accounts and abuse the platform.

## Solution
Require a valid payment method (credit card) even for free trials using Stripe Setup Intents ($0 authorization).

## Implementation
1. Create Stripe Setup Intent on signup
2. Require successful card validation before enabling evolutions
3. Store payment method for future conversion to paid
4. Consider allowing limited functionality (view-only) without card

## Benefits
- Biggest single deterrent to abuse
- Higher conversion rates (card already on file)
- Can charge for overages if needed

## Alternative
If card requirement is too aggressive, consider:
- Phone verification instead
- Hard limit of 3 total evolutions (not per month) for unverified accounts

## Files to modify
- `ace_platform/api/routes/auth.py`
- `ace_platform/core/stripe_integration.py` (or create new)
- Frontend signup flow

title: Rate limit account creation per IP
description:
## Problem
No rate limit on account creation allows rapid creation of throwaway accounts from a single IP.

## Solution
Add rate limiting to the registration endpoint.

## Implementation
1. Add rate limit: 3 accounts per hour per IP
2. Use existing Redis-backed rate limiting infrastructure in `ace_platform/core/rate_limit.py`
3. Return 429 with clear message about limit

## Files to modify
- `ace_platform/api/routes/auth.py` - add rate limit decorator to register endpoint
- `ace_platform/core/rate_limit.py` - add REGISTER limit config if needed

## Impact
Prevents rapid-fire account creation for abuse.

title: Implement password reset flow
description:
## Problem
No password reset mechanism exists. Users who forget their password have no recovery path.

## Solution
Implement secure password reset with email verification.

## Implementation
1. Add `/auth/forgot-password` endpoint - accepts email, sends reset link
2. Add `/auth/reset-password` endpoint - accepts token + new password
3. Generate secure reset tokens (cryptographic random, short expiry)
4. Store token hash in database (not plaintext)
5. Token expiry: 1 hour
6. Invalidate token after use
7. Rate limit reset requests (3 per hour per email)

## Security considerations
- Don't reveal whether email exists (same response either way)
- Use timing-safe comparison for tokens
- Log all reset attempts for audit

## Files to modify
- `ace_platform/api/routes/auth.py`
- `ace_platform/db/models.py` - add PasswordResetToken model
- Add email sending utility

title: Add security headers middleware
description:
## Problem
Missing standard security headers that browsers use for protection.

## Solution
Add middleware to set security headers on all responses.

## Headers to add
- `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains` (HSTS)
- `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`
- `X-Frame-Options: DENY`
- `X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block`
- `Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin`
- `Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'` (adjust as needed for API)

## Implementation
1. Create middleware in `ace_platform/api/middleware.py`
2. Add to middleware stack in `ace_platform/api/main.py`
3. Make headers configurable (some may need adjustment for frontend)

## Notes
- Less critical for API-only service but still good practice
- HSTS especially important for production

title: Implement audit logging for security events
description:
## Problem
Security events are not formally audited. No way to investigate incidents or meet compliance requirements.

## Solution
Add structured audit logging for security-relevant events.

## Events to log
- Login success/failure (with IP, user agent)
- Password changes
- API key creation/revocation
- Email verification
- Password reset requests
- Account lockouts
- Permission denied events
- Subscription changes
- Admin actions

## Implementation
1. Create `ace_platform/core/audit.py` with audit log functions
2. Create AuditLog database model with:
   - timestamp, user_id, event_type, ip_address
   - user_agent, details (JSON), severity
3. Add audit calls to relevant endpoints
4. Add admin endpoint to query audit logs
5. Consider shipping to external logging service (optional)

## Files to modify
- `ace_platform/db/models.py` - add AuditLog model
- `ace_platform/core/audit.py` - new file
- Various route files to add audit calls

title: Add two-factor authentication (2FA) support
description:
## Problem
High-value accounts are vulnerable to credential compromise. No second factor of authentication.

## Solution
Add optional 2FA using TOTP (Google Authenticator, Authy, etc.)

## Implementation
1. Add 2FA fields to User model:
   - `totp_secret` (encrypted)
   - `totp_enabled`
   - `backup_codes` (hashed)
2. Add endpoints:
   - `POST /auth/2fa/setup` - generate secret, return QR code
   - `POST /auth/2fa/verify` - verify code, enable 2FA
   - `POST /auth/2fa/disable` - disable with password confirmation
   - `GET /auth/2fa/backup-codes` - generate backup codes
3. Modify login flow to require 2FA code when enabled
4. Add backup codes for recovery

## Libraries
- `pyotp` for TOTP generation/verification
- `qrcode` for QR code generation

## Alternative
Consider just pushing users toward Google/GitHub OAuth which provides 2FA at the provider level - simpler and shifts security responsibility.

title: Add device/session management
description:
## Problem
- No tracking of active sessions or devices
- No way to see where you're logged in
- Can't revoke specific sessions
- JWT tokens can't be invalidated before expiry

## Solution
Add session tracking and management.

## Implementation
1. Create Session model:
   - id, user_id, token_hash, device_info
   - ip_address, user_agent, created_at, last_used_at
   - is_revoked
2. Store session on login, update last_used on each request
3. Add endpoints:
   - `GET /auth/sessions` - list active sessions
   - `DELETE /auth/sessions/{id}` - revoke specific session
   - `DELETE /auth/sessions` - revoke all except current
4. Check session validity on each authenticated request
5. Add Redis-based token blocklist for immediate revocation

## Tradeoffs
- Adds database lookup on every request (can cache in Redis)
- More complex auth flow
- Consider if really needed for your user base
